summary: Russian forces reported the liberation of Okhrimovka in Kharkiv region and Roskoshnoe in the DPR. Ukrainian forces destroyed a drone production workshop in the Chuguev area and damaged four bridges near the Crimean entrance at Armянськ. Air defenses over Moscow and in Rostov region shot down dozens of hostile drones.
key_points: Russian army announced liberation of Okhrimovka village in Kharkiv region and Roskoshnoe village in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone production workshop in the Chuguev area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukrainian forces damaged four bridges near Armянськ on the approach to Crimea, including two over the North‑Crimean Canal (Source: @UkraineNow/81836).
15 Shahed drones were reported operating in the Black Sea heading toward the South‑Black Sea area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67571).
Ukrainian drones attacked a cargo ship in the Azov Sea, killing four Azerbaijani citizens and one Russian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30839).

Russian night attack on a railway station in Sumy region killed a 44‑year‑old woman and injured another; separate attacks in Mykolaiv and Kyiv region caused fires and vehicle damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Moscow’s air defence system destroyed seven drones heading for the capital, and over 60 drones were shot down in Rostov region during one night (Source: @tass_agency/380340; @tass_agency/380343).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces announced the liberation of Okhrimovka in Kharkiv and Roskoshnoe in the DPR, while Ukrainian forces destroyed a drone workshop in Chuguev and damaged four bridges near the Crimean entrance at Armянськ. Air defences over Moscow and in Rostov region intercepted dozens of hostile drones, and a Ukrainian drone strike in the Azov Sea killed multiple civilians. Nighttime Russian attacks continued in Sumy, Mykolaiv and Kyiv regions, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.

points: Ukrainian forces used a Geran drone strike to destroy a Russian‑controlled drone production workshop in the Chuguev area, reducing enemy UAV manufacturing capability (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).

Four bridges on the approach to Crimea near Armянськ were hit, including two spanning the North‑Crimean Canal and a road bridge toward Perkopp‑Armянськ, disrupting Russian logistical routes (Source: @UkraineNow/81836).

Ukrainian drones attacked a cargo vessel in the Azov Sea, resulting in the deaths of four Azerbaijani citizens and one Russian, demonstrating the reach of Ukrainian UAV operations into contested waterways (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30839).
Ukrainian air defence units over Moscow reported shooting down seven incoming drones, reflecting coordinated defensive actions beyond Ukrainian territory (Source: @tass_agency/380340).
The Russian army announced the liberation of Okhrimovka village in Kharkiv region and Roskoshnoe village in the DPR, indicating territorial gains on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).

Russian forces conducted a night raid on a railway station in Sumy region, killing a 44‑year‑old woman and wounding another civilian, showing continued use of kinetic strikes against infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
In Mykolaiv, a Russian UAV attack caused a large fire, damaged two cars and destroyed fourteen private houses, highlighting the impact on civilian settlements (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).

Over 60 hostile drones were intercepted and destroyed in Rostov region during a single night, demonstrating intensive Russian air‑defence activity against aerial threats (Source: @tass_agency/380343).
Ukrainian forces reported destroying a drone production workshop in the Chuguev area, a location within the eastern operational zone (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Russian liberation of Okhrimovka village in Kharkiv region marks a shift in control on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
Ukrainian UAV sightings reported near Izium and near the border of Kharkiv and Sumy regions indicate ongoing reconnaissance activity (Source: @kpszsu/64860; @kpszsu/64901).
Russian air‑defence reported destroying more than 60 drones in Rostov region, a key area adjacent to the eastern front (Source: @tass_agency/380343).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over Sumy region, including near Shostka and Glukhov, suggesting continued aerial surveillance in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/96284; @eRadarrua/96282).
Russian night attack on a railway station in Sumy resulted in civilian casualties, indicating active combat operations on the northern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Ukrainian forces observed a Shahed drone presence in the Black Sea heading toward the southern‑Black Sea area, which may affect northern maritime routes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67571).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near Chernihiv (Gorodnyu/Snovsk) approaching from the north, highlighting cross‑border aerial movements (Source: @eRadarrua/96300).
Ukrainian drones attacked a cargo ship in the Azov Sea, killing civilians and demonstrating operational reach on the southern maritime front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30839).
Fifteen Shahed drones were observed operating in the Black Sea toward the South‑Black Sea sector, posing a threat to southern coastal installations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67571).
Ukrainian forces reported damage to four bridges near the Crimean entrance at Armянськ, affecting Russian supply lines on the southern front (Source: @UkraineNow/81836).
Russian UAV attacks in Mykolaiv caused extensive fire and property damage, underscoring continued pressure on southern Ukrainian cities (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Moscow’s air‑defence system shot down seven drones inbound to the capital, showing effective interception capabilities (Source: @tass_agency/380340).
More than 60 drones were destroyed in Rostov region during a single night, indicating high‑intensity aerial engagements (Source: @tass_agency/380343).

Ukrainian UAVs were reported operating over the Black Sea, Sumy, Chernihiv, and near the border of Kharkiv and Sumy, reflecting widespread aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/64860; @kpszsu/64901; @eRadarrua/96284).
Russian Shahed drone activity was noted in the Black Sea, with fifteen units heading toward the South‑Black Sea area (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67571).

key_developments: Russian forces announced the liberation of Okhrimovka village in Kharkiv region and Roskoshnoe village in the DPR, indicating territorial gains (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
A Russian Geran drone destroyed a Ukrainian drone production workshop in the Chuguev area, reducing Ukraine's UAV manufacturing capacity (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30837).
Ukrainian forces damaged four bridges near Armянськ on the approach to Crimea, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81836).
Fifteen Shahed drones were reported operating in the Black Sea toward the South‑Black Sea sector, posing a maritime threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67571).
Ukrainian drones attacked a cargo ship in the Azov Sea, killing four Azerbaijani citizens and one Russian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30839).
Russian night attacks on a railway station in Sumy region killed a civilian and caused additional casualties in Mykolaiv and Kyiv regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81854).
Moscow’s air defence destroyed seven drones heading for the capital, and over 60 drones were shot down in Rostov region during one night (Source: @tass_agency/380340; @tass_agency/380343).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are consolidating gains in the east, notably in Okhrimovka and Roskoshnoe, while maintaining pressure on civilian infrastructure through night raids. Ukrainian UAV capabilities remain active, targeting Russian logistics, maritime assets, and production facilities, but face increasing air‑defence challenges as Moscow and regional commands intercept large numbers of drones. The presence of Shahed drones in the Black Sea adds a persistent maritime threat to southern ports and naval operations.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine continues to leverage UAV strikes to disrupt Russian supply lines and target strategic workshops, while seeking to protect critical bridge infrastructure near Crimea. Russian authorities are intensifying air‑defence measures around major cities and employing night attacks to undermine Ukrainian morale and infrastructure. International actors are monitoring the escalation, particularly the use of drones in contested maritime zones.
sections.intelligence_notes: Further monitoring of UAV activity in the Black Sea and along the eastern front is essential to anticipate potential strikes on shipping and logistics hubs. The reported destruction of a drone workshop in Chuguev suggests a focus on degrading Ukrainian UAV production; additional sites may be targeted. Russian liberation claims should be corroborated with satellite imagery to assess actual control on the ground.
